title: Remove the ClearLink (CLINK) Controller
summary: Remove the ClearLink (CLINK) controller from its controller box for replacement or service by applying lockout/tagout, using required PPE and tools, accessing the controller area if needed, releasing the controller box, disconnecting the controller cables, and removing the controller from the box.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when the ClearLink (CLINK) controller must be removed from the controller box for replacement or service.
not_for: Do not use this procedure when the required Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure is not available.
Do not use this procedure for normal operator tasks.
Do not use this procedure as a reinstallation or replacement instruction beyond controller removal.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, access the controller area, release the controller box, disconnect cables, and remove the CLINK controller.

safety_notes: Use the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure before accessing or removing the controller.
Do not remove the four M8 button-head screws when releasing the controller box; loosen them and lift the box off the loosened fasteners.
access_or_tools_needed: 3/8-in. wrench or socket if side panel removal is necessary

system_preconditions: Tipper is locked out/tagged out using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
Controller area is accessible, including station side panel removal if needed.

visual_references.description: Figure identifying the CLINK controller and controller box location for removal and replacement.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe state before accessing the controller area.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure before beginning controller removal.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out and tagged out and safe to access for service.
steps.healthy_condition: LOTO has been applied in accordance with the referenced procedure before any panel or controller hardware is accessed.
steps.failure_condition: The lockout/tagout procedure is unavailable or LOTO cannot be completed.

actions.label: Apply LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The referenced lockout/tagout procedure is not available.
LOTO cannot be completed as required.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
Use LOTO as required by the source before accessing or removing the controller.

steps.title: Put on required PPE
steps.purpose: Ensure the technician is wearing the PPE specified for this removal task.
steps.instruction: Put on safety glasses and gloves before performing the removal procedure.
steps.expected_result: Required PPE is in place before work continues.
steps.healthy_condition: Safety glasses and gloves are worn.
steps.failure_condition: Required PPE is not available or not worn.

actions.label: Wear PPE
actions.exact_action: Put on the listed PPE: safety glasses and gloves.

source_evidence.text: Put on the listed PPE: safety glasses and gloves.
Nearby text states required tools include a 2-mm hex wrench, wire tie cutting tool, and wire ties, with estimated time of 15 minutes and PPE of safety glasses and gloves.

steps.title: Gather required tools and parts
steps.purpose: Prepare the listed tools and replacement part before opening the equipment.
steps.instruction: Gather the replacement CLINK controller, 2-mm hex wrench, tool to cut wire ties, wire ties, and if necessary a 3/8-in. wrench or socket.
steps.expected_result: All listed tools and parts are available for the removal task.
steps.healthy_condition: The required tools and replacement part are present before proceeding.
steps.failure_condition: One or more required tools or parts are missing.

actions.label: Collect tools and parts
actions.exact_action: Gather the listed tools and parts: replacement CLINK controller, 2-mm hex wrench, tool to cut wire ties, wire ties, and if necessary a 3/8-in. wrench or socket.

stop_or_escalate_if: Required tools or parts are not available.

source_evidence.text: Gather the listed tools and parts: replacement CLINK controller, 2-mm hex wrench, tool to cut wire ties, wire ties, and if necessary a 3/8-in. wrench or socket.
Nearby text states required tools include a 2-mm hex wrench, wire tie cutting tool, and wire ties, with estimated time of 15 minutes and PPE of safety glasses and gloves.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel if access is needed
steps.purpose: Open access to the controller area when the side panel blocks access.
steps.instruction: If necessary, remove the nine self-tapping fasteners from the station side panel with a 3/8-in. wrench or socket, then remove the station side panel to access the area.
steps.healthy_condition: The side panel is removed only if needed and the controller area can be reached.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel cannot be removed or access to the controller area is still blocked.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Remove the nine self-tapping fasteners from the station side panel with a 3/8-in. wrench or socket.

actions.label: Remove side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the CLINK controller figure to identify the controller box area and access location after removing the station side panel.

stop_or_escalate_if: The side panel cannot be removed safely.
Access to the controller area remains blocked after panel removal.

source_evidence.text: If necessary, remove the nine self-tapping fasteners from the station side panel with a 3/8-in. wrench or socket, then remove the station side panel to access the area.

The procedure instructs technicians to LOTO the tipper, remove the station side panel if needed, loosen four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box, and disconnect the cables connected to the CLINK controller.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-13 appears on page 157 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_13_1_removal.

steps.title: Release the controller box
steps.purpose: Free the controller box from its mounting points so the controller can be accessed and removed.
steps.instruction: Loosen the four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box. Do not remove these fasteners. Lift the box off the loosened fasteners.
steps.expected_result: The controller box is released from its mounting fasteners and can be lifted free.
steps.healthy_condition: The controller box lifts off after the four M8 button-head screws are loosened, while the fasteners remain in place.
steps.failure_condition: The controller box cannot be released after loosening the four M8 button-head screws.

actions.label: Loosen controller box screws
actions.exact_action: Loosen the four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box.

actions.label: Lift box off fasteners
actions.exact_action: Do not remove these fasteners; lift the box off the loosened fasteners.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Controller box mounting location and the four M8 button-head screw attachment points.

stop_or_escalate_if: The controller box cannot be released after loosening the four M8 button-head screws.
The box does not lift free from the loosened fasteners.

source_evidence.text: Loosen the four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box. Do not remove these fasteners; lift the box off the loosened fasteners.

Escalate if the controller box cannot be released after loosening the four M8 button-head screws, if cable connections cannot be safely disconnected, or if the source-referenced lockout/tagout procedure is not available.

The procedure instructs technicians to LOTO the tipper, remove the station side panel if needed, loosen four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box, and disconnect the cables connected to the CLINK controller.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-13 appears on page 157 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_13_1_removal.

steps.title: Disconnect the CLINK controller cables
steps.purpose: Separate the controller from connected cabling before removing it from the box.
steps.instruction: Disconnect the cables connected to the CLINK controller.
steps.expected_result: All cables connected to the CLINK controller are disconnected.
steps.healthy_condition: The controller is free of connected cables and ready for removal from the box.
steps.failure_condition: Cable connections cannot be safely disconnected or remain attached.

actions.label: Disconnect controller cables
actions.exact_action: Disconnect the cables connected to the CLINK controller.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Identify the CLINK controller and the connected cables to be disconnected.

stop_or_escalate_if: Cable connections cannot be safely disconnected.
The connected cables cannot be clearly identified from the available source material.

source_evidence.text: Disconnect the cables connected to the CLINK controller.

Escalate if the controller box cannot be released after loosening the four M8 button-head screws, if cable connections cannot be safely disconnected, or if the source-referenced lockout/tagout procedure is not available.

The procedure instructs technicians to LOTO the tipper, remove the station side panel if needed, loosen four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box, and disconnect the cables connected to the CLINK controller.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-13 appears on page 157 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_13_1_removal.

steps.title: Remove the controller from the box
steps.purpose: Complete removal of the CLINK controller so it can be replaced or serviced.
steps.instruction: Remove the two M5 socket-head fasteners holding the controller in the box and lift the controller out.
steps.expected_result: The CLINK controller is removed from the controller box and lifted out.
steps.healthy_condition: The controller is free of the box after the two M5 socket-head fasteners are removed.
steps.failure_condition: The controller cannot be freed from the box or cannot be lifted out after fastener removal.

actions.label: Remove controller fasteners
actions.exact_action: Remove the two M5 socket-head fasteners holding the controller in the box.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Controller seated in the box and the fastener locations used to secure it.

stop_or_escalate_if: The controller cannot be removed after the two M5 socket-head fasteners are removed.
The controller remains mechanically bound in the box.

source_evidence.text: Remove the two M5 socket-head fasteners holding the controller in the box and lift the controller out.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-13 appears on page 157 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_13_1_removal.

success_criteria: The CLINK controller is removed from the controller box.
The controller has been lifted out for replacement or further service.
healthy_conditions: The tipper is locked out/tagged out before work begins.
Required PPE and tools are available.
The controller box releases after loosening the four M8 button-head screws without removing them.
The controller lifts out after the two M5 socket-head fasteners are removed.
failure_conditions: The referenced lockout/tagout procedure is not available.
The controller box cannot be released after loosening the four M8 button-head screws.
Cable connections cannot be safely disconnected.
The controller cannot be removed from the box.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the source-referenced lockout/tagout procedure is not available.
Escalate if the controller box cannot be released after loosening the four M8 button-head screws.
Escalate if cable connections cannot be safely disconnected.
Because this procedure involves LOTO, panel removal, hardware removal, and controller disconnection in the tipper area, it should be handled by L2 support rather than an operator.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: CLINK controller and controller box location for access, release, cable disconnection, and controller removal.
